positiveChecks: spec_coverage: ran `npm test` live — 558/558 pass (0 fail), matching the claimed count in state.json/execution-notes.md. Grepped the ENTIRE src/ tree for readFileSync/readdirSync/existsSync/import.meta.url beyond cost-model.ts: the only other runtime fs users are src/shell/wallet.ts, src/shell/qr.ts, src/shell/budget-store.ts (all resolve via `path.join(os.homedir(), '.blockrun')` — a user-home config dir, never a repo/src-relative path) and src/cli/json-flag.ts (reads a user-supplied `@file.json` CLI arg). scripts/generate-cost-model.mjs and scripts/docs-check.mjs are dev-only (scripts/ is not in package.json's `files` field, never shipped). No other runtime dependency on files absent from the npm package exists.

money_path_invariance: confirmed the parity test (test/unit/cost-model-generated-parity.test.ts) is a genuine independent re-derivation, not a tautological copy — proved this by LIVE mutation: temporarily renamed chat.ts's `gatePaidCall(` call to `gatePaidCallXXX(` (simulating drift) and ran the parity test in isolation; it correctly FAILED with `chat: 'paid' (committed) vs 'free' (fresh re-derivation)`. Reverted the mutation, confirmed `git status` clean and full `npm test` 558/558 green again. Free/paid classification is unchanged from the pre-fix runtime derivation: wallet/models/dex = free, the other 15 = paid, verified both by static inspection of src/core/cost-model.generated.ts and live `node dist/index.js commands --json` output from a REAL extracted npm-pack tarball (18 entries, exact match).

backward_compatibility: src/core/cost-model.ts is a thin re-export (`export { COMMAND_COST_MODEL, type CostModel } from "./cost-model.generated.js"`) — public API unchanged, so commands-catalog.ts and other consumers need no changes (confirmed none were touched). `git diff dbb2033(feature init)..HEAD --stat` scope is sane and matches the spec exactly: package.json (+1 prebuild line), scripts/generate-cost-model.mjs (new), src/core/cost-model.generated.ts (new), src/core/cost-model.ts (rewritten), test/cli/packed-tarball.test.ts (new), test/unit/cost-model-generated-parity.test.ts (new), plus VCSDD bookkeeping/evidence files — no unrelated files touched. Audited commit bbd7303 (`git show bbd7303 --stat`): it introduced scripts/generate-cost-model.mjs and src/core/cost-model.generated.ts alongside the phase 1a/1b spec commit; `git diff bbd7303..HEAD` on both files is EMPTY (byte-identical) — no divergence between the parallel-commit content and the final shipped state, coherent throughout.

real_binary_behavior: performed the full real flow myself, not trusting the builder's report. `npm run build` — prebuild hook fired (`node scripts/generate-cost-model.mjs` printed 'wrote 18 entries'), tsup bundled dist/index.js; `git status --short src/core/cost-model.generated.ts` showed NO diff after regeneration (deterministic, no drift). `npm pack --pack-destination <tmp>` produced a real .tgz with 'total files: 4'; extracted via `tar xzf` into an isolated tmp dir — contents were exactly `package/{LICENSE,README.md,package.json,dist/index.js}`, confirming the shipped package truly contains only those 4 items. With HOME pointed at a fresh throwaway directory (no repo relationship), from inside the extracted package dir: `node dist/index.js --version` -> stdout `1.2.0`, exit 0. `node dist/index.js commands --json` -> exit 0, valid JSON, `commands.length === 18`, free/paid values exactly matching the spec's KNOWN_CORRECT set. `node dist/index.js wallet --action status --json` -> exit 0; verified stdout is a SINGLE clean JSON line (`{"activeChain":"base",...}`) with the onboarding welcome banner correctly routed to stderr, not stdout. `node dist/index.js rpc --network "../bad" --method x --json` -> exit 2, `{"error":true,"code":"usage_error",...}`. Stale-generation protection verified live (see money_path_invariance entry above) by deliberately introducing then reverting a real source mutation and observing the parity test catch it.
